# Expeditors Data Scientist Salaries in Seattle
...
The average Expeditors Data Scientist in Seattle earns an estimated $164,412 annually, which includes an estimated base salary of $136,908 with a $27,504 bonus. Expeditors' Data Scientist compensation is $34,907 more than the US average for a Data Scientist. Data Scientist salaries at Expeditors in Seattle can range from $91,000 - $280,000.
...

results.title: James M.
highlights:
Software Engineer - GUIDE Team at [Expeditors](https://www.linkedin.com/company/expeditors)
...
14+ years building business-critical software across logistics, e-commerce, travel, compliance, and real estate technology. I specialize in modernizing legacy platforms, designing reliable backend/data workflows, and helping teams turn complex technical problems into clearer, more reliable systems. My strongest work sits at the intersection of systems engineering, product judgment, and operational leverage: compliance rules engines, accessibility automation, cloud/platform modernization, search optimization, CI/CD cost reduction, developer workflows, and AI-assisted tooling. I’m comfortable moving between architecture, hands-on implementation, stakeholder communication, mentoring, and delivery execution. Earlier sales and real estate experience shaped how I think about software: the best systems do not just function technically — they help people make better decisions, move faster, and understand tradeoffs more clearly. Recent focus areas include AI-enabled internal tools, Python/FastAPI, React/TypeScript, Java/Spring, Postgres, AWS, data normalization, prompt-driven workflows, and practical human-in-the-loop systems. I’m especially interested in teams building workflow automation, data products, platform tools, developer experience, logistics/supply-chain systems, healthcare systems, or real estate/fintech technology. Leadership style: direct communication, shared ownership, high-trust teams, practical engineering standards, and a bias toward reducing ambiguity quickly. I’m targeting Senior Software Engineer, Technical Lead, AI Product Engineer, and EM-track roles where I can stay close to the code while helping teams ship durable systems and grow into broader ownership.
...

results.title: Expeditors' 47-Year Streak Just Broke. 230 Walk Aug 8, And You Have 60 Days. · Refolk
highlights:
On June 8, 2026, Expeditors International filed a WARN notice ending a 47-year no-layoff tradition, releasing 230 technologists from its U.S. Global Technology Department across five Washington offices. First separations land Aug 8, with the rest spread through Dec 31. If you recruit supply-chain software talent in the Puget Sound, this is the cleanest, most time-boxed liquidity event you will see this year, and the window to act started the day the notice was signed.
...
Role mix, per laid-off employees talking to GeekWire and KIRO 7: software developers, QA testers, project managers, and business analysts. A "vast majority" are developers. Notice date is June 8, first separations Aug 8, all wrapped by Dec 31.
...
That culture produced engineers who have spent 10, 20, sometimes 30 years inside one of the largest freight-forwarding platforms on the planet. They know customs brokerage workflows, EDI and AS2 plumbing, ocean and air manifest integration, and the inside of the U.S. Customs ACE system at a depth that AI-native logistics startups simply cannot hire elsewhere.
...

...

...

...
find outside legacy 3PLs just walked into the open market on a published calendar.
...
Run your outreach against current Expeditors employees in the affected job families, in the five named cities, with tenure of 5+ years. Describing that filter in a Boolean string on LinkedIn Recruiter is painful and brittle. Describing it in English is fast, which is why we built Refolk: you ask in plain English ("software engineers and QA at Expeditors in Seattle, Federal Way, Lynnwood, Bellevue, or Airway Heights, 5+ years tenure") and get a ranked shortlist across GitHub, LinkedIn, and the open web in one pass.
...
Washington ESD's Rapid Response program kicks in automatically at WARN filings. They will run resume workshops and job fairs for these 230 employees. Show up. Sponsor sessions. CSCMP's Puget Sound Roundtable, FreightWaves' reader community, and The Loadstar are the other channels that index on supply chain software talent specifically, rather than generic tech.
...
itors-specific
...
- June 8, 2026: WARN filed. Notice delivered. Outreach window opens.
- June 8 to Aug 7: Best window. Engineers still employed, unencumbered by severance paperwork, most receptive to outbound.
- Aug 8, 2026: First separations. LinkedIn "Open to Work" tags start appearing. Public competition begins.
- Aug 8 to Dec 31: Rolling separations through year-end. Second-tier candidates still available, but pricing has corrected upward.
- Dec 31, 2026: Cuts complete. The 47-year streak is officially a historical footnote.
...
The 60 days from notice to first separation

Immediately. The WARN notice was June 8 and first separations are Aug 8, which means there is a roughly 60-day window where targeted employees are still employed, have not signed severance or non-solicit paperwork, and are most receptive to outbound. Waiting until "ex-Expeditors" tags appear on LinkedIn in August means you are competing for the second tier.
...
Per the WARN filing and laid-off employees speaking to GeekWire and KIRO 7, the mix is software developers, QA testers, project managers, and business analysts, with a "vast majority" being developers. The deeper value is domain expertise in freight forwarding, customs brokerage, EDI/AS2 integration, and global trade workflows that's hard to find outside legacy 3PLs.

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist III job with Expedia Group | 1417143
highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist III job with Expedia Group | 1417143
...
# Machine Learning Scientist III
...
Employer Expedia Group Location Bangalore, India Salary Competitive Closing date 30 Jul 2026
...
Sector Data Science, Science and Pharmaceutical Role Professional Contract Type Permanent Hours Full Time
...
## Job Details
...
At Expedia Group, we help travelers explore the world, one journey at a time. As a global travel company powered by passionate people, trusted partnerships, and leading technology, we connect travelers, partners, and advertisers through our consumer brands, B2B network, and travel advertising business.Here, you'll do meaningful work that helps millions of people discover, book, and experience travel with more ease, confidence, and joy. Our five Behaviors-Traveler First, Think Big, Operate with Excellence, Ownership Mindset, and Succeed Together-help foster a supportive environment where people can grow their careers and have the flexibility, benefits, and support to do their best work. Join us and build for travelers everywhere.Introduction to teamWe create and deliver tailored marketing strategies for Expedia Group`s brands, focusing on establishing strong connections and cohesive experiences for travelers and partners. We leverage our functional expertise and creative excellence to build trust and loyalty for our brands through innovative marketing approaches and technology.Are you passionate about using Machine Learning (ML) and Natural Language Processing techniques to enhance the Traveler Experience? Our Marketing Content Machine Learning Science team focuses on generating and optimizing content that delivers relevant, personalized experiences to travelers and drives value for our business.If you're ready for your next challenge and want to innovate in a forward-thinking and cutting-edge team, we want to speak with you!We are looking for a self-motivated and independent individual who will have end to end ownership over driving impact to Expedia`s largest Marketing channels. With ample access to the latest technology this could be an exciting position for someone who loves to explore and deeply understand applications of machine learning!In this role, you will:
...
- Develop and implement advanced machine learning models (including Large Language Models) and statistical algorithms, processing massive datasets to build scalable prototypes.
- Explore new content opportunities in text and image domains to create personalized traveler experiences with high business impact.
...
- Design and run experiments (A/B testing, multi-variate, factorial design, stratified sampling) to measure the effectiveness of campaigns and new initiatives.
...
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including Machine Learning Science, Marketing, and Engineering, and present findings to executives and stakeholders to drive strategic decisions.
- Contribute to the success of Expedia's ML community by mentoring team members, fostering a collaborative culture, and sharing best practices with other ML teams.
...
Experience and Qualifications:
...
- Master`s Degree or PhD preferred in a related field, or equivalent related professional experience
- Proficient in one or more data science programming languages (Python, Java, R).
- Proven experience applying NLP techniques and LLMs in production to solve business problems and generate significant value.
- Strong expertise in ML techniques such as Bayesian Methods, Clustering, Decision Trees, Latent Dirichlet Allocation, NER, Random Forests, and more.
- Accustomed to collaborative, fun work environments.

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist III at Expedia | OnNetPulse
highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist III at Expedia | OnNetPulse
...
# Machine Learning Scientist III at Expedia
...
Data Science & Analytics Automation & AI Engineering
...
The EG Advertising Machine Learning team sits at the heart of Expedia Group’s global advertising marketplace—a fast-growing, high-impact business at the intersection of travel, technology, and commerce. The team develops the predictive models that power our platform, including click-through rate and booking propensity models, as well as deeper insights into advertiser bidding behaviour and broader marketplace dynamics. It’s a unique environment where machine learning directly shapes real-time auctions, personalised experiences, and large-scale optimisation across a complex two-sided ecosystem.
...
As a Machine Learning Scientist III, you’ll play a key role within this team, applying machine learning to connect millions of travellers with thousands of partners. One of the most exciting aspects of the role is the end-to-end visibility across both auction and personalisation systems, allowing you to drive meaningful, system-wide impact rather than focusing on a single component. Working closely with product and engineering partners, you’ll combine deep data exploration with strong business context to design and deliver scalable, production-ready solutions that directly influence user experience and marketplace performance.
...
Design, develop, and apply machine learning, statistical, and optimization models to solve business problems in EG Advertising, including predictive modelling for ad auction systems (e.g., click-through rate, booking propensity, and advertiser bidding dynamics), improving both partner and traveller outcomes.
...
Translate ambiguous business questions into measurable scientific
...
Partner closely with engineers, product managers, analysts, and business stakeholders to productionize models, influence roadmap decisions, and drive adoption of ML-powered solutions across both partner-facing and traveller-facing systems.
...
Build and evaluate scalable approaches across multiple technical domains, including feature engineering, model development, experimentation design (A/B testing), data modelling, and integration into production services within large-scale distributed systems.
...
Safely integrate and operate AI/ML-enabled solutions that improve outcomes, including applying modern ML techniques and selective use of GenAI/agentic AI approaches to enhance modelling, experimentation, and system capabilities.
...
Advanced degree (MS or PhD) in Machine Learning, Computer Science, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Economics, or a related field.
...
5+ years of relevant industry experience applying machine learning, statistical modelling, experimentation, or optimisation techniques in production environments.
...
Demonstrated ownership of end-to-end machine learning solutions at the service, multi-service, or domain level, with accountability for model quality, business impact, and operational reliability.
...
Strong technical foundation in machine learning methods, experimental design (A/B testing), data analysis, and programming for production-quality solutions working with large-scale datasets.
...
Proficiency in Python and its ML/data ecosystem (e.g., PySpark, pandas, TensorFlow, PyTorch or similar) and strong SQL skills.
...

Experience building and scaling machine learning systems for advertising, ranking, recommendation, auction optimisation, pricing, or forecasting.

highlights:
Machine Learning Engineer III at Expedia | OnNetPulse
...
# Machine Learning Engineer III at Expedia
...
## Job Details
...
PostedJun 25, 2026
...
## About the Role
...
We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer III to join the Expedia Group B2B Applied Machine Learning Team. You will be part of a team that designs, builds, and maintains the infrastructure for deploying ML solutions, managing data pipelines, and optimizing compute resources for our ranking problems, recommendation engines, and pricing optimization systems. Your work will be crucial in ensuring our ML models are efficiently deployed, scaled, and monitored in production environments, contributing substantial value to our partners and EG.
...
We're seeking a passionate and experienced ML engineer to join our Revenue Optimization ML team at Expedia Group. This high-impact role combines infrastructure expertise with applied ML knowledge, requiring proficiency in software engineering, distributed systems, and machine learning operations (MLOps). You will be responsible for architecting and implementing robust ML systems, collaborating with data scientists to streamline the model development to production pipeline, and optimizing our ML infrastructure for performance and scalability.
...
This role is well-suited to someone with a solid foundation in at least one of the following areas: software engineering, data engineering, distributed systems, or applied machine learning and a readiness to grow into others as the work requires. Experience or exposure to one or more of the following is valuable: deploying models to production, managing training data pipelines, or optimizing compute resources for low latency inference. We’re looking for someone who can quickly adapt to new technologies, understand the broader context of our ML systems, and deliver high-quality solutions efficiently. While this is primarily an individual contributor role, you will also be expected to mentor junior team members and provide technical guidance that drives both business impact and team growth.
...
In this role, you will:
...
- Design and implement scalable ML infrastructure for training, deployment, and serving in batch and real-time environments.
- Build and maintain efficient data pipelines for large-scale processing and feature engineering.
- Optimize compute resources and improve model serving performance across ML systems.
- Implement robust monitoring, logging, and alerting systems, and contribute to ML Ops practices including CI/CD pipelines.
- Collaborate closely with ML scientists to streamline the model development-to-production workflow.
- Research and integrate new technologies, mentor junior engineers, and communicate technical solutions to diverse stakeholders.
...
Experience and qualifications:
...
- You hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field.
- You have 3+ years of experience in Software, Data, or ML engineering roles (preferred).
- You bring strong problem-solving skills, proficiency in Python, and experience with ML frameworks like TensorFlow or PyTorch.
- You understand ML algorithms, model architectures, and enjoy building scalable, reliable ML systems.
- You have exposure to cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP), containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and scalable data systems (e.g., Spark, Kafka).
- You are familiar with CI/CD tools (e.g., GitHub Actions), ML model serving technologies (e.g., MLflow), and collaborate well across teams.
...

Our Technology Team partners with teams across Expedia Group to create innovative products, services, and tools to deliver high-quality experiences for travelers, partners, and our employees. A singular technology platform powered by data and machine learning provides secure, differentiated, and personalized experiences that drive loyalty and traveler satisfaction.

Expedia Global Payments (EGP) is looking for top engineering talent to help drive the development of our payment platform, seamlessly roll out new payment methods, and continuously improve on our operational excellence and engineering practices. We offer a highly dynamic set of services that enable customers from all over the world to pay for travel in a variety of ways. We build, operate, and improve payment services for Expedia Group's brands, our suppliers (hoteliers, airlines, vacation rental owners/agencies, etc.), and our corporate needs. We do this accurately and at scale, processing over $100 billion payments in 55+ currencies in over 155 countries every year, and across a diverse set of payment options! This opportunity presents great technical challenges and the ability to have a tremendous business impact.

highlights:
[Remote] Machine Learning Scientist III | Jobflarely
...
# [Remote] Machine Learning Scientist III
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Expedia Group is a leader in travel technology, and they are seeking a Machine Learning Scientist III to join their Traveler Engagement & Loyalty team. The role focuses on building and optimizing machine learning models for customer relationship management, directly influencing traveler experiences through personalized recommendations and engagement strategies. Responsibilities • Build and optimize recommendation models for CRM use cases, including personalized email campaigns and retention strategies • Apply deep learning and causal inference to sparse, noisy, and dynamic customer interaction data • Design and analyze A/B experiments to validate model performance and drive product decisions • Collaborate with ML Engineers to deploy models at scale, ensuring robustness and reliability in production environments • Translate business objectives into measurable machine learning goals aligned with customer engagement outcomes • Stay current with advancements in recommendation research, deep learning architectures, and personalization systems Skills • MS, or BS in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Statistics, Engineering, or a related field; or equivalent professional experience • 3+ years of experience building production-grade recommendation or personalization systems • Strong understanding of collaborative filtering, deep learning for recommendations, reinforcement learning, and causal inference • Proficiency in Python and ML frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch • Experience with distributed systems like Apache Spark • Familiarity with A/B testing and experimentation methodologies • Ability to work with large-scale, real-world data with attention to bias, fairness, and privacy • Excellent communication skills for both technical and non-technical audiences • PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Statistics, Engineering, or a related field • Experience in the travel or e-commerce industry • Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) • Publications in top-tier ML conferences or journals • Contributions to open-source ML projects • Experience in personalization systems or CRM-focused ML applications • Experience taking models from prototype to production in collaboration with Machine Learning Engineering teams Company Overview • At Expedia Group (NASDAQ: EXPE), we believe travel is a force for good – it opens minds, builds connections, and bridges divides. It was founded in 1996, and is headquartered in Seattle, Washington, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Expedia Group has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 519 in 2025, 410 in 2024, 382 in 2023, 629 in 2022, 483 in 2021, 366 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

# AI Developer – Container & Logistics Industry

Remote, USA

Posted 2026-06-18

Full-time
...
## We are seeking a skilled  AI Developer  to help modernise and optimise operations across the container and logistics ecosystem. You will build intelligent systems that enhance container tracking, route optimization, yard operations, demand forecasting, predictive maintenance, and supply chain visibility. This role focuses on applying AI to real-world shipping and containerized freight challenges — improving operational efficiency, reducing costs, and increasing transparency across global logistics networks. What You Will Be Doing:  Design and deploy AI/ML models for: Container tracking and ETA prediction Route and load optimization Yard and terminal operations automation Predictive maintenance for container fleets and equipment Demand forecasting and capacity planning Develop real-time analytics systems for supply chain monitoring Integrate AI solutions with TMS (Transportation Management Systems), WMS (Warehouse Management Systems), and IoT devices Work with telematics, GPS, RFID, and sensor data Build APIs and scalable services to support operational platforms Collaborate with operations, logistics planners, and engineering teams Ensure data security and compliance across global shipping regulations Optimise models for production-scale performance About You:  Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, AI, Data Science, or related field 3+ years of experience in AI/ML development Strong Python programming skills Experience with machine learning frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn) Experience working with time-series data and large operational datasets Knowledge of optimization algorithms (linear programming, heuristic optimization) Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) Familiarity with REST APIs and microservices architecture Understanding of MLOps and model deployment Your Qualifications: Experience in logistics, shipping, freight, or container operations Familiarity with supply chain analytics and transportation systems Experience with IoT data processing Knowledge of geospatial analytics (GIS tools, mapping APIs) Experience building real-time streaming systems (Kafka, Spark, etc.) Understanding of global trade operations and port logistics Experience working in distributed, remote teams What We Offer: Fully remote, flexible work environment Competitive salary with performance incentives Opportunity to work on AI systems impacting global trade and logistics Access to large-scale operational datasets Professional development and industry exposure Collaborative, cross-functional international team Impact You’ll Make: Improve container turnaround time Reduce empty container repositioning costs Increase supply chain visibility Optimize port and yard efficiency Support sustainable and data-driven logistics operations
